title: teucrium compactum subsp. rixanense
intro: Teucrium compactum subsp. rixanense, also known as the Alpine Germander, is a subspecies of perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Alpine Germander include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Alpine Germander is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
